rationale.R1.1: Section makes three explicit load-bearing contribution claims (COW multi-version index, O(1) amortized read, BALD-style scheduler) whose coherence with the stated gap requires verification; thesis reconstruction is central but not the single failure mode.
rationale.R1.2: The bulk of the section consists of technical design arguments—size-tiered vs. leveled compaction, O(log N) read-amplification tolerance, scheduler-to-storage dependency chain—whose internal logical validity is the primary object of any technical review; failure here invalidates the section.
rationale.R1.3: The 38% annotator-hours claim is forward-referenced to Section 5 without confidence intervals or scope caveats; the acquisition-function choice (BALD-style) is asserted without experimental grounding in this section; evidence-claim linkage is live.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty is explicitly asserted against Delta Lake (row-vs-cell versioning), O'Neil-style LSM (provenance as first-class dimension), and implicitly against BALD-style active learning applied outside the annotation-storage context; all three deltas require scrutiny.
rationale.R1.7: Six citation anchors appear (O'Neil et al., Delta Lake, Cassandra, RocksDB, Steinhardt et al., Houlsby et al.), several used in 'in the spirit of' constructions that may mischaracterize scope; attribution accuracy and coverage are live concerns.
